name: SNIPE associated domain
description: This entry represents the SNIPE associated domain, a small largely α-helical domain found in the SNIPE protein family. SNIPE proteins cleave phage DNA during genome injection. This domain may help SNIPE bind to phage DNA.

name: Spindle pole component Bbp1, N-terminal
description: This N-terminal domain of Bbp1, a spindle pole body component, interacts directly, though transiently, with the polo-box domain of Cdc5. Full length Bbp1 localises at the cytoplasmic side of the central plaque periphery of the spindle pole body (SPB) and plays an important role in inserting a duplication plaque into the nuclear envelope and assembling a functional inner plaque [[cite:PUB00070000]]. Although not a membrane protein itself, Bbp1 binds to Mps2 as well as to Spc29 and the half-bridge protein Kar1, thus providing a model for how the SPB core is tethered within the nuclear envelope and to the half-bridge [[cite:PUB00070001]].

name: Spindle pole body component Bbp1, C-terminal
description: This C-terminal domain of Bbp1, a spindle pole body component, carries coiled-coils that are necessary for the localisation of Bbp1 to the spindle pole body (SPB) [[cite:PUB00070000]]. Although not a membrane protein itself, Bbp1 binds to Mps2 as well as to Spc29 and the half-bridge protein Kar1, thus providing a model for how the SPB core is tethered within the nuclear envelope and to the half-bridge [[cite:PUB00070001]].

name: SNIPE associated domain
description: This entry represents the SNIPE associated domain a small largely alpha helical domain found in the SNIPE protein family. SNIPE proteins cleave phage DNA during genome injection. This domain may help SNIPE bind to phage DNA.

name: Meiotically up-regulated gene 113
description: This is a family of fungal proteins found to be up-regulated in meiosis.
